summary: boolean value handling bug?
Aggregate DBQueryResult gets "unblessed reference" error
Default write concern should not send a "w" value
description: A user should be allowed to set an empty "w" value for a write concern.  In such a case, no "w" should be transmitted to the server, causing the server to use its default value instead.

summary: Optimize fetching all results from a query result
description: Calling "all" on a query result winds up iterating item by item to build up a response.  However, in the v1.x branch, it should be possible to pull off all result documents in one go before getting the next batch.  That should reduce function call overhead dramatically.

summary: cursor created by function aggregate is slower than cursor created by function find
description:
When I compare the actual time it takes to retrieve an object using cursor->next with the Perl driver the result clearly shows that cursor created by the find function is twice as fast as a cursor created by the aggregate function.

I've verified that find and aggregate return exactly the same data so the issue is not the amount of data they return.
The query is verify simple:

find({ env => 'TEST'})->sort({hostname => 1});
aggregate( [ { '$match' => { env => 'TEST' } }, { '$sort' => { hostname => 1 } } ] );

The total number of records in the collection is 6635 and the query return 5095 of them.

Why is the cursor created by aggregation twice as slow as the one created by find?

summary: Document loss of precision when encoding long doubles
description: Perl can be compiled with long double support, but BSON encoding works by casting Perl's internal value to double.  -This can overflow.  We should detect this situation and throw an error.-

summary: Versions v1.0.0 and v1.0.1 fail to build on CentOS 6.3.
description:
Perl driver versions v1.0.0 and v1.0.1 fail to build on CentOS 6.3.

I tried v1.0.1 first, then added/upgraded some modules with below and tried again:
{noformat}
cpanm CPAN::Meta DateTime::Tiny Test::Harness Time::Moment IO::Socket::IP IO::Socket::SSL Mozilla::CA IO::Socket::SSL
{noformat}

I then tried v1.0.0, and it also failed. They all failed 13 tests.

Build logs and perl -V attached.

summary: Use electionId to detect stale primaries.
Set slaveOK for getMore/killCursors
Easier way to find by _id
Add 'batch' method to QueryResult
description: Right now, the QueryResult class gives the option of 'next' or 'all'.  A useful middle ground would be 'batch', which would give the current cursor batch as a list (fetching new batches as necessary).

summary: possible improvement to IndexView
description:
when create index, if there is one already exists.

there are two cases, 

1. both keys and options are the same. create_one works fine.

question:  I am not sure it is recreated or just ignore the re-recreate request.

2.  keys are the same, but options are different. create_one will die.
a database error is thrown. 
{noformat}
'MongoDB::DatabaseError: exception: Index with name: okay_1_name_-1 already exists with different options'
{noformat}

The error seems too general. if I want to get the duplicate index name, have to use regex to extract and drop it. Maybe a new subclass of DatabaseError?

another way is to iterate all the know indexes and find the one is exact the same with the one to be created, but each index is a hash. compare equal is not straight forward as I wish.

maybe there should be an api to check if an index exists or not?

Thanks.

summary: Uses LLONG_(MIN|MAX) without including limits.h
description:
{noformat}
03:10:02.184 perl_mongo.c: In function 'sv_to_bson_elem':
03:10:02.184 perl_mongo.c:676: error: 'LLONG_MAX' undeclared (first use in this function)
03:10:02.185 perl_mongo.c:676: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only once
03:10:02.185 perl_mongo.c:676: error: for each function it appears in.)
03:10:02.185 perl_mongo.c:676: error: 'LLONG_MIN' undeclared (first use in this function)
{noformat}

On other OSes (e.g. Debian Jessie, CentOS 6) it happens to work, presumably because some other header pulls in `limits.h`

summary: Check findAndModify results for write concerns
description:
Per Craig Wilson:

{quote}
When the writeConcern on findAndModify fails, the resulting document
still comes back with ok: 1. This is the same as the write commands.
Hence, to check for write concern and raise the appropriate error to
your users, you will need to check the resulting document for a
writeConcernError field. If it is present, then writeConcern failed.
{quote}

summary: Update doesn't return a MongoDB::UpdateResult object
Install of v1.1.0-TRIAL fails
description: Install fails, see attached log file.

summary: Still "can't get db response" in MongoDB perl driver
Create stub MongoDB::GridFSBucket class
description: Add a MongoDB::GridFSBucket class with required options in the [GridFS spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#configurable-gridfsbucket-class] and supporting tests.

summary: Implement GridFSBucket drop
description: Implement the [bucket drop|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#dropping-an-entire-gridfs-bucket] API from the GridFS spec.

summary: Implement GridFSBucket generic find
description: Implement the [Generic  Find|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#generic-find-on-files-collection] API from the GridFS spec.

summary: Implement GridFSBucket file upload
description:
Implement the [File Upload|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#file-upload] API from the GridFS spec.

This will also require implementing a MongoDB::GridFSBucket::UploadStream class.

summary: Implement GridFSBucket file deletion
description: Implement the [File Deletion|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#file-deletion] API from the GridFS spec.

summary: Implement GridFSBucket file download
description:
Implement the [File Download|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#file-download] API from the GridFS spec.

This will also require implementing a MongoDB::GridFSBucket::DownloadStream class.

summary: Add support for legacy Cpanel::JSON::XS booleans
description:
MongoDB-v1.3.0-TRIAL:
- i686-linux-thread-multi / 5.18.1:
  - FAIL http://www.cpantesters.org/cpan/report/ac16d10a-a5cf-11e5-95fc-cf9a233d5411

Error:
{noformat}
During encode_one, type (Cpanel::JSON::XS::Boolean) unhandled at /home/c/.cpan/build/MongoDB-v1.3.0-hSu_Qw/blib/lib/MongoDB/BSON.pm line 215.
 at t/bson_codec/booleans.t line 61.
    # Child (class Cpanel::JSON::XS) exited without calling finalize()

#   Failed test 'class Cpanel::JSON::XS'
#   at /home/c/perl5/perlbrew/perls/18.1i/lib/5.18.1/Test/Builder.pm line 276.
# Tests were run but no plan was declared and done_testing() was not seen.
# Looks like your test exited with 255 just after 6.
t/bson_codec/booleans.t ..... 
Dubious, test returned 255 (wstat 65280, 0xff00)
Failed 1/6 subtests 
{noformat}

Hypothesis: this is an old Cpanel::JSON::XS before it standardized its handling of booleans.  Waiting on answer from CPAN Tester to clarify which version was in use.

summary: Use setVersion and electionId to detect stale primaries
description: See DRIVERS-279.

summary: BSON config checks unused defines
description: The BSON configuration checks BSON_WITH_OID32_PT and BSON_WITH_OID64_PT but these are no longer used by libbson.  Possibly, they need to be replaced with the inverse logic for the presence of atomics.  (If atomics don't exist then libpthread is required).

summary: BSON memory leak on error
description:
The BSON reader allocated in BSON.xs isn't freed if an exception occurs.

The deallocation function should probably be scheduled via {{SAVEDESTRUCTOR}} so that it runs on scope unwind.

summary: Support maxTimeMS option in parallelScan helpers
description: See DRIVERS-295

summary: Memory leak in GridFSBucket::UploadStream without Class::Accessor installed
description:
Perl MongoDB-1.4.0 brought t/gridfs_spec.t and t/gridfsbucket.t tests. Both of these tests report insufficient memory and fails because of that:

t/gridfs.t .................. ok
Out of memory!
Out of memory!
t/gridfs_spec.t ............. 
All 6 subtests passed 
Out of memory!
Out of memory!
t/gridfsbucket.t ............ 
All 4 subtests passed 
t/indexview.t ............... ok
[...]
Test Summary Report
-------------------
t/gridfs_spec.t           (Wstat: 139 Tests: 6 Failed: 0)
  Non-zero wait status: 139
  Parse errors: No plan found in TAP output
t/gridfsbucket.t          (Wstat: 139 Tests: 4 Failed: 0)
  Non-zero wait status: 139
  Parse errors: No plan found in TAP output

Maximum RSS memory consumed by the test is 4191428 KB.

The tests pass on 64-bit x86_64 machine that has the same amount of memory.

When running the test directly I can see this output:

+ FAILPOINT_TESTING=1
+ /usr/bin/time perl -Iblib/lib -Iblib/arch t/gridfs_spec.t
    # Subtest: Upload when length is 0
        # Subtest: Compare collections
        ok 1 - No extra chunks in fs.chunks
        ok 2 - No extra chunks in expected.chunks
        ok 3 - files[1]._id (*result = &result)
        ok 4 - files[1].chunkSize
        ok 5 - files[1].filename
        ok 6 - files[1].length
        ok 7 - files[1].md5
        ok 8 - files[1].uploadDate (Passing with special *actual value)
        ok 9 - No extra files in fs.files
        ok 10 - No extra files in expected.files
        1..10
    ok 1 - Compare collections
    1..1
ok 1 - Upload when length is 0
    # Subtest: Upload when length is 1
Out of memory!
Out of memory!
302.22user 4.60system 5:11.14elapsed 98%CPU (0avgtext+0avgdata 4190444maxresident)k
0inputs+0outputs (0major+1054712minor)pagefaults 0swaps

I suspected I had not enough memory. But because it works on x86_64 I believe it's not because of the memory. Probably something odd is in 32-bit code. Maybe the mongod server exhausts the memory, so there is not enough for the Perl test.

The server uses mmapv1 storage with smallFiles enabled on the x86.

summary: t/gridfsbucket.t never finishes without Class::XSAccessor installed
description:
If Class::XSAccessor is not installed t/gridfsbucket.t  test does not finish:

$ perl -Iblib/{lib,arch} t/gridfsbucket.t
ok 1 - read preference
ok 2 - write concern
ok 3 - default bucket name
ok 4 - default chunk size bytes  
ok 5 - upload small file
ok 6 - upload small file has 1 chunk
ok 7 - upload small file chunk n 
ok 8 - upload small file data
ok 9 - upload small file files document
ok 10 - upload small file md5
ok 11 - upload small file length 
ok 12 - upload small file length 
ok 13 - upload small file uploadDate
ok 14 - upload large file
    # Subtest: upload large file 
    ok 1 - upload large file chunk 0 n
    ok 2 - upload large file chunk 0 data
    ok 3 - upload large file chunk 1 n
    ok 4 - upload large file chunk 1 data
    ok 5 - upload large file chunk 2 n
    ok 6 - upload large file chunk 2 data
    ok 7 - upload large file chunk 3 n
    ok 8 - upload large file chunk 3 data
    ok 9 - upload large file chunk 4 n
    ok 10 - upload large file chunk 4 data
    1..10
ok 15 - upload large file
ok 16 - upload large file whole file
ok 17 - upload large file files document
ok 18 - upload large file md5
ok 19 - upload large file length
ok 20 - upload large file filename
ok 21 - upload large file uploadDate
ok 22 - upload large file metadta
ok 23 - upload large file content_type
ok 24 - upload large file aliases
ok 25 - bucket delete files
ok 26 - bucket delete chunks
ok 27 - delete nonexistant file
ok 28 - found file length
ok 29 - only one document found
ok 30 - drop leaves files empty
ok 31 - drop leaves chunks empty
ok 32 - new bucket doesn't create indexes
ok 33 - unique files index on filename+uploadDate created
ok 34 - unique chunks index on files_id+n created
ok 35 - upload on same bucket doesn't reindex
ok 36 - upload on same bucket doesn't reindex
^C

It never completed the "download_to_stream does not close file handle" test.

A symptom is the test uses up all disk size (a temporary file in /tmp) and the code tries to write the data again and again without bailing out on the I/O error:

write(3, "The Project Gutenberg EBook of T"..., 8192) = -1 ENOSPC (No space left on device)
write(3, "The Project Gutenberg EBook of T"..., 8192) = -1 ENOSPC (No space left on device)
write(3, "The Project Gutenberg EBook of T"..., 8192) = -1 ENOSPC (No space left on device)
[...]

If I install Class::XSAccessor, the tests passes on the same system.

summary: Document new BSON type wrappers
Ensure serializing integers larger than LLONG_MAX is an error
Add support for serializing 'u' and 'l' regex flags
description: Native perl regexes support 'u' and 'l', but the driver will drop them on serialization.

summary: Extract pure-perl BSON methods to BSON::PP
description: Split the pure-perl methods into a separate file.  This will make it clearer what is the common API (in BSON.pm) and what is the pure-Perl encoder/decoder that parallels the BSON::XS implementation.

summary: Document pure-Perl BSON changes
description: Need to document behavior changes

summary: Regex compilation should escape problematic characters
description: The {{try_compile}} method in MongoDB::BSON::Regexp doesn't escape any characters, which could cause compilation problems.

summary: ReadConcern level must be lowercase when sent to a server
description: Per the [read concern spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/read-write-concern/read-write-concern.rst#read-concern], read concern level must be lower-cased.

summary: Using $literal in $projection
description:
I get an error as below whenever I try to use the $literal operator in a projection. Is this this operator is supported by the driver version I am using? Can somebody provide an example if this operator is supported by the driver version I am using?

exception: invalid operator $literal

summary: Error applying read preferences for mongos in topology mode single
Query modifiers incorrectly translated into find command
description: Query modifiers (except '$hint') are not properly translated when converted to {{find}} command form.

summary: Refactor internal operation classes and roles
description:
The current use of roles with internal operation classes is a bit confusing, with names that don't clearly reflect their actual role and some operations applying roles for these unstated effects.  For example, having {{MongoDB::Op::_FindAndUpdate}} apply the {{MongoDB::Role::_ReadOp}} role is odd since find-and-modify is a write operation.

Roles should be reviewed and rationalized.   As part of this, common attributes could be moved from operations into roles to remove duplication and ensure consistency.

summary: Clean Up Maybe[ReadPreference] in ReadOp
description: nonstandard code path uses undef type for read preference instead of mocking the object.  Should be cleaned up to mirror code path for ReadConcern

summary: Driver won't authenticate with hidden servers
Implement maxStalenessMS
description: See DRIVERS-293

summary: Implement BSON::OID::from_epoch
description:
This tracks the corresponding community PR on github: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-perl-bson/pull/2

This change will improve compatibility with the Mango driver.

summary: Legacy update method does not allow the update to be empty
Allow index tests which use invalid options to fail with server error
description:
As of 3.4.0-rc0, the server no longer accepts arbitrary index options (SERVER-769).

The "ensure index arbitrary options" driver tests should be updated accordingly.

summary: Implement BSON::XS support in BSON.pm with fallback to pure-perl
description: Loading BSON.pm should try to load BSON::XS and use its methods if available.  Otherwise, it should fall back to the pure-perl methods.

summary: Implement filter option for collection_names
description: The listCollections spec says that associated methods must have the ability to filter results.

summary: Don't warn on unknown regex flags
description:
The "incompatible regex flag stripped" warnings may be undesirable in some situations, like when regexes are pre-compiled elsewhere for use both inside perl and in mongo queries (in particular the /u flag), so you just want them to be stripped silently. Let's add a category via warnif so they can be silenced with 'no warnings qw(...)'

Looks like it should either be 'regexp' or something like 'MongoDB::regex'
